title: Email Volume Anomaly
spl:
index=security tag=email
| timechart span=1h count
| predict count as prediction algorithm=LLP future_timespan=0
| where count > 2*prediction
description: Spike in `Email` model message count may indicate spam flood, phish campaign, or misconfigured relay.
value: Spike in `Email` model message count may indicate spam flood, phish campaign, or misconfigured relay.
implementation: Seasonal business hours in `predict`; whitelist marketing bursts via subject lookup.
visualization: Line chart (actual vs. predicted), Single value (ratio).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Mail-volume and content shifts from marketing pushes, O365 throttling, mailbox migrations, and journaling changes; not every spike is a phishing wave.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Exchange, Proofpoint → Email.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `Email` CIM.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Seasonal business hours in `predict`; whitelist marketing bursts via subject lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=email
| timechart span=1h count
| predict count as prediction algorithm=LLP future_timespan=0
| where count > 2*prediction
```

Understanding this SPL

**Email Volume Anomaly** — Spike in `Email` model message count may indicate spam flood, phish campaign, or misconfigured relay.

Documented **Data sources**: `Email` CIM. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Exchange, Proofpoint → Email. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Pipeline stage (see **Email Volume Anomaly**): predict count as prediction algorithm=LLP future_timespan=0
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 2*prediction` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Email Volume Anomaly** — Spike in `Email` model message count may indicate spam flood, phish campaign, or misconfigured relay.

Documented **Data sources**: `Email` CIM. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Exchange, Proofpoint → Email. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Email.All_Email` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (actual vs. predicted), Single value (ratio).
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Email Volume Anomaly" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Email.All_Email` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
